# FINALIZE_GOVERNED_EXECUTION_RUNTIME_V1

## Status

Frozen and certified.

This milestone certifies the first bounded governed execution runtime:

`Human -> Browser Companion -> Governed Intent Interpretation -> Governed Codex Task Synthesis -> Replay-certified Handoff Package -> Execution Authorization Gate -> Execution Consumer -> Governed Codex Execution Adapter -> Bounded Codex Execution -> Replay-certified Execution Receipt`

## Certified Included Components

1. Browser Companion
2. Local Preview Runtime
3. Intent Interpretation Layer
4. Codex Task Synthesis Layer
5. Codex Handoff Package Layer
6. Execution Authorization Gate
7. Execution Consumer
8. Codex Execution Adapter
9. Authority Token Validation
10. Replay Identity Chain
11. Explicit Human Approval Model
12. Bounded Codex Dispatch
13. Deterministic Execution Receipts
14. stdout/stderr hashing
15. `shell=False` enforcement
16. bounded timeout enforcement

## Constitutional Statement

The system can perform bounded governance-controlled Codex execution, but does not perform autonomous execution.

## Certified Exclusions

- autonomous execution
- orchestration
- retries/fallbacks
- hidden continuation
- recursive planning
- worker coordination
- general agent runtime
- unrestricted Codex execution
- unrestricted subprocess execution
- `shell=True`
- hidden networking
- automatic escalation
- implicit approvals
- persistent execution authority
- public runtime exposure
- self-modifying governance

## Closure Statement

The first bounded governed execution runtime epoch is closed. Further execution
expansion requires a new constitutional review, not an implicit extension.
